per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, there's no sweating or anything like that. It's just a general feeling of anxiety and discomfort in my chest. (pauses) Actually, now that you mention it, I did feel a little sweaty during one of the episodes yesterday, but it was mild and only lasted for a few minutes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, not really. Just a little bit like I said, during that one episode yesterday. But nothing significant. It's mostly just this tightness in my chest and feeling winded. (pauses) And to be honest, it's been going on for so long now, even when I'm sitting still or lying down, which is pretty unusual for me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The tightness stays right in my chest, usually just below the breastbone. It's a bit hard to describe, but it feels like pressure building up inside there. Sometimes it feels like it's radiating out to my left arm, but that's not always the case. It's pretty localized to my chest area most of the time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I mentioned earlier that sometimes it feels like it's radiating out to my left arm, but only during the more severe episodes. It's not a sharp pain or anything, just a kind of tingling sensation in my arm when the pressure builds up in my chest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I did mention that earlier, didn't I? Yeah, during the more severe episodes, I get this tingling sensation in my left arm. It's not a big deal, but it's definitely there and can be a bit unsettling.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I don't feel anything out of the ordinary in my feet or legs, just some mild swelling in my ankles that's been going on for a while. It's nothing too serious, just feels like my shoes are a bit tighter than usual because of it.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I mentioned earlier that I've had some mild swelling in my ankles, but other than that, I don't notice anything unusual. It's just that my shoes feel a bit tighter than usual because of it. Nothing too concerning, just kind of annoying, you know?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, my shoes definitely feel tighter than usual, and I've noticed that my ankles are a bit puffy, especially when I wake up in the morning. It's not super painful, just kind of uncomfortable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yeah, sometimes I get a little bit of nausea during those episodes. It's not like I'm going to throw up or anything, but it's just this weird feeling in my stomach that goes along with the chest tightness and shortness of breath. But it's not always there, maybe half the time or so?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I'd say about half the time, I get this mild nausea that goes along with the chest tightness and shortness of breath. It's not like a full-blown stomachache or anything, just a weird, queasy feeling that passes after the episode is over.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad had a heart attack when he was 52 years old, unfortunately. He passed away from it. My mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's still alive and doing okay at 78. That's about it in terms of family medical history that I'm aware of.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, my dad had a heart attack when he was 52 years old. He didn't survive it. It's been on my mind a lot lately, to be honest, given what I'm going through with my chest tightness and shortness of breath.
